title: Egyptian Egg Roll Gyoza Delight
description: Rich and velvety Egyptian-inspired egg rolls filled with spherified egg and Mediterranean-friendly ingredients, perfect for a sick-day comfort food
instructions: Prepare spherified egg by mixing egg whites with 1 tablespoon of water and sodium alginate. Hold dropper of mixture above water bath and release slowly.
Form gyoza dough with remaining egg yolks and 1/2 cup of water. Knead until smooth.
Combine feta cheese, olives, artichoke hearts, parsley, garlic, salt, and black pepper in a bowl.
Assemble gyoza by placing a tablespoon of the cheese mixture onto the center of a gyoza wrapper, then brush with water and fold in half to seal.
Fry gyoza in a large skillet with 1 cup of vegetable oil over medium-high heat until golden brown.
Drain fried gyoza on paper towels and serve hot with a side of honey glaze for dipping (if using).
To spherify egg, use a syringe or piping bag to inject the egg mixture into cold water and wait for 5 minutes before serving.
Repeat the frying process until all gyoza are cooked and reserve on a paper-lined plate.
To serve, place a few spherified egg pieces on top of fried gyoza and garnish with chopped parsley.
